hero.subtitle: Supporting parents in the magical journey of child development from birth to 5 years

learning.title: Interactive Learning Center
learning.subtitle: Engaging content to develop your child's knowledge
learning.focus_helper: These activities match the developmental stage and keep play simple, safe, and repeatable.
learning.activities_helper: Switch age groups to see a fresh set of play ideas for the day.
learning.activity_hint: Tap a card to keep exploring
learning.parent_intro: Use short sessions, follow your child's attention, and repeat the same game across the week to build confidence.
learning.daily_rhythm_text: Try one activity in the morning, one after rest, and one before bedtime. Keep each session under 10 minutes.
learning.summary_0_1: Gentle play that supports bonding, sensory tracking, and first movements.
learning.summary_1_2: Movement and early language games that build confidence and curiosity.
learning.summary_2_3: Hands-on play that grows vocabulary, problem solving, and coordination.
learning.summary_3_4: Creative tasks that strengthen counting, emotions, and fine motor control.
learning.summary_4_5: Pre-school style activities that prepare children for reading, writing, and teamwork.
learning.focus_0_1: Bonding, visual tracking, and gentle movement
learning.focus_1_2: Walking, naming, and simple object play
learning.focus_2_3: Language growth, sorting, and pretend play
learning.focus_3_4: Early math, scissors, and emotional learning
learning.tip_0_1_a: Watch your baby's cues and stop if they look tired or overstimulated.
learning.tip_0_1_b: Use soft voices, calm eye contact, and safe toys with different textures.
learning.tip_0_1_c: Repeat the same activity in short sessions to make it familiar.
learning.tip_1_2_a: Let your child try first and help only when they need it.
learning.tip_1_2_b: Use simple words while naming objects, actions, and colors.
learning.tip_1_2_c: Praise small efforts to build confidence and independence.
learning.tip_2_3_a: Offer two choices instead of many to keep play easy to follow.
learning.tip_2_3_b: Mix movement with language so the child learns through doing.
learning.tip_2_3_c: Keep toys visible and easy to reach for independent exploration.
learning.tip_3_4_a: Use games that ask the child to sort, count, and name what they see.
learning.tip_3_4_b: Let mistakes happen during craft or movement play and guide gently.
learning.tip_3_4_c: Talk about feelings during play to support emotional growth.
learning.tip_4_5_a: Include letters, numbers, and story questions in everyday play.
learning.tip_4_5_b: Encourage turn-taking so children practice cooperation and patience.
learning.tip_4_5_c: Ask your child to explain what they made or learned in their own words.
learning.activity_tummy_time_desc: Short supervised tummy time to build neck and shoulder strength.
learning.activity_contrast_cards_desc: Bold black-and-white patterns support early visual tracking.
learning.activity_soft_sound_play_desc: Gentle rattles and music help listening and calm focus.
learning.activity_face_talk_desc: Talk, smile, and mirror expressions to build communication.
learning.activity_reach_grasp_desc: Offer safe toys for reaching, holding, and exploring with hands.
learning.activity_stacking_cups_desc: Stack and knock down cups to practice hand control and problem solving.
learning.activity_point_name_desc: Point to animals, objects, and body parts while naming them together.
learning.activity_walk_chase_desc: Encourage walking after bubbles or a rolling ball to build movement confidence.
learning.activity_simple_songs_desc: Sing repeating songs with hand motions to support language and rhythm.
learning.activity_sort_big_small_desc: Sort safe toys by size, color, or type with your help.
learning.activity_color_match_desc: Match toys, blocks, or cards by color to strengthen early thinking skills.
learning.activity_pretend_cooking_desc: Toy utensils and play food build imagination and vocabulary.
learning.activity_ball_toss_desc: Roll or toss a soft ball back and forth to improve coordination.
learning.activity_picture_story_desc: Look at picture books and ask simple questions about what you see.
learning.activity_two_steps_desc: Give two-step directions like “pick up the toy and put it in the box.”
learning.activity_shape_hunt_desc: Find circles, squares, and triangles around the home or classroom.
learning.activity_cut_paste_desc: Practice safe scissor skills with paper strips and glue sticks.
learning.activity_counting_games_desc: Count toys, steps, or snacks together up to 10 and beyond.
learning.activity_emotion_faces_desc: Name happy, sad, angry, and calm faces to grow emotional awareness.
learning.activity_obstacle_course_desc: Create a simple indoor course with pillows, tunnels, and crawling spaces.
learning.activity_letter_hunt_desc: Search for letters in books, signs, and labels around the house.
learning.activity_story_retell_desc: Ask your child to retell a story using beginning, middle, and end.
learning.activity_pattern_blocks_desc: Build and copy patterns with blocks, beads, or colored paper.
learning.activity_name_writing_desc: Trace letters, draw lines, and practice writing first name with support.
learning.activity_team_games_desc: Play turn-taking games that teach patience, sharing, and cooperation.
